name: Roast Duckling a La Orange
recipeIngredient: 2 cups brown sauce or chicken stock
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Joe Mannke of Rotisserie for Beef and Bird, Houston, TX Preparation Time: 2:00
STEP ONE: Prepare the Duckling and Basic Brown Sauce.
Trim the duck of excess fat at the base of the tail. Rub the inside with the seasoned salt and rosemary.
Preheat oven to 450 degrees F (232 degrees C).
Place the duckling in a roasting pan, add 1/2 cup of water, and roast for 1-1/2 hours, basting occasionally. Remove the duckling from the oven and keep warm.
Pour off all but two tablespoons of fat from the roasting pan, add the onions and celery, and sauté until tender. Add two cups of brown sauce. Boil for 20 minutes.
The basic sauce is now ready for additional flavoring.
STEP TWO: Prepare the Orange Sauce.
Melt the butter, vinegar, and sugar in a saucepan over medium heat. Caramelize the liquid until it begins to boil. Add the orange juice, mustard, and basic brown sauce, then bring to a boil again.
Strain the sauce into a bowl, then return it to the pan. Thicken with cornstarch and season with Grand Marnier.
